name: REST API Service
description: A standalone REST API service with typed endpoints, authentication middleware, database integration, OpenAPI documentation, and production deployment. Use this for backend services consumed by frontends or other services.

phases.name: Setup
phases.description: Bootstrap the API project with a production-grade server framework, TypeScript configuration, database connection, and development tooling.

epics.name: Server Bootstrap
epics.description: Initialize the project with a Node.js/TypeScript server and verify it starts and responds to health checks.

stories.title: Initialize Node.js TypeScript project with Fastify
stories.description: Create the package.json, tsconfig.json (strict mode), and install Fastify with its TypeScript types. Configure ts-node-dev for hot-reload development.
completion_criteria: `pnpm dev` starts the server and `curl localhost:3000/health` returns HTTP 200 with `{"status": "ok"}`
TypeScript strict mode is enabled and `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0 on the initial scaffold

stories.title: Configure structured logging with Pino
stories.description: Configure Fastify's built-in Pino logger with JSON output in production and pretty-print in development, controlled by the NODE_ENV environment variable.
completion_criteria: In NODE_ENV=production mode, every request log line is valid JSON parseable by `jq`
The log includes requestId, method, url, statusCode, and responseTime fields on every request

epics.name: Database Integration
epics.description: Connect to PostgreSQL, configure the ORM, and run the initial schema migration.

stories.title: Connect Drizzle ORM to PostgreSQL
stories.description: Install Drizzle ORM and drizzle-kit, configure the database connection pool using the DATABASE_URL environment variable, and verify the connection on server startup.
completion_criteria: The server logs a 'Database connected' message on startup and fails with a clear error if DATABASE_URL is missing or the database is unreachable
The connection pool is capped at 10 connections (configurable via DB_POOL_MAX environment variable)

stories.title: Define initial schema and run first migration
stories.description: Create the initial Drizzle schema file, generate the SQL migration, and verify it applies cleanly to a fresh database.
completion_criteria: `npx drizzle-kit push` applies the schema to the local database without errors
The migration is idempotent: running it twice against the same database does not produce errors

epics.name: Developer Experience
epics.description: Set up linting, formatting, testing framework, and contribution documentation.

stories.title: Configure ESLint, Prettier, and Vitest
stories.description: Add ESLint with TypeScript rules, Prettier for consistent formatting, and Vitest as the test runner with a basic test that verifies the setup.
completion_criteria: `pnpm test` runs the sanity test and exits 0
`pnpm lint` exits 0 on the initial codebase

phases.name: Core Endpoints
phases.description: Implement the primary business logic as typed, validated REST endpoints with proper error handling.

epics.name: Request Validation Layer
epics.description: Establish input validation so all endpoints enforce schemas on request bodies, query strings, and path parameters.

stories.title: Integrate Zod validation with Fastify schema compiler
stories.description: Replace Fastify's default JSON schema validator with a Zod-based compiler so route schemas can be defined as Zod objects and TypeScript types are inferred automatically.
completion_criteria: A route with a Zod body schema rejects requests with invalid body fields with HTTP 400 and a structured error listing the invalid fields
The inferred TypeScript type for `request.body` matches the Zod schema without any `as` cast

epics.name: Primary Resource CRUD
epics.description: Implement the full Create-Read-Update-Delete lifecycle for the service's primary domain resource.

stories.title: Implement POST /resources — create endpoint
stories.description: Validate the request body with Zod, insert the record into the database via Drizzle, and return the created resource with HTTP 201.
completion_criteria: POST /resources with a valid body returns HTTP 201 and the full created resource including its generated id and createdAt timestamp
POST /resources with a missing required field returns HTTP 400 with a body listing the specific missing fields

stories.title: Implement GET /resources (paginated list) and GET /resources/:id
stories.description: List endpoint returns cursor-based paginated results. Single-item endpoint returns 404 when the resource does not exist.
completion_criteria: GET /resources returns at most 20 items and includes a `nextCursor` field when more items exist
GET /resources/:id with an id that does not exist returns HTTP 404 with body `{"error": "Resource not found"}`

stories.title: Implement PATCH /resources/:id and DELETE /resources/:id
stories.description: Partial update endpoint merges provided fields onto the existing record. Delete endpoint hard-deletes the record and returns HTTP 204.
completion_criteria: PATCH /resources/:id with only a `name` field updates only the name and leaves all other fields unchanged
DELETE /resources/:id returns HTTP 204 and a subsequent GET for the same id returns HTTP 404

phases.name: Auth
phases.description: Secure the API endpoints with JWT Bearer token authentication and role-based access control.

epics.name: JWT Middleware
epics.description: Implement authentication middleware that validates Bearer tokens on protected routes.

stories.title: Implement JWT verification middleware as Fastify plugin
stories.description: Create a Fastify plugin that verifies the Authorization Bearer token on every route decorated with `preHandler: [authenticate]`. Attach the decoded payload to `request.user`.
completion_criteria: A request to a protected route without an Authorization header returns HTTP 401 with body `{"error": "Authentication required"}`
A request with a valid JWT has `request.user.sub` set to the user id from the token payload

stories.title: Add POST /auth/token endpoint for machine-to-machine tokens
stories.description: Implement a client credentials endpoint that accepts client_id and client_secret and returns a short-lived access token for service-to-service API calls.
completion_criteria: POST /auth/token with valid client credentials returns a JWT with `exp` set to 1 hour from now
POST /auth/token with an invalid client_secret returns HTTP 401

phases.name: Testing
phases.description: Write a comprehensive test suite covering the API contract, error paths, and edge cases.

epics.name: API Contract Tests
epics.description: Use supertest or Fastify's `inject` method to test every endpoint against a real test database.

stories.title: Write contract tests for the primary CRUD endpoints
stories.description: Cover all HTTP methods on the primary resource: happy-path responses, validation error responses, 404 responses, and auth rejection.
completion_criteria: All CRUD contract tests pass with `pnpm test`
Test coverage for src/routes/ is above 90% as reported by `pnpm test --coverage`

stories.title: Write contract tests for the auth endpoints
stories.description: Test the token issuance endpoint with valid credentials, invalid credentials, and missing fields.
completion_criteria: Tests cover: valid credentials return a token, invalid secret returns 401, missing client_id returns 400
Tests verify the returned token can be used to access a protected endpoint

phases.name: Documentation
phases.description: Generate OpenAPI documentation, write a developer guide, and document the deployment process.

epics.name: OpenAPI Specification
epics.description: Auto-generate the OpenAPI spec from the route schemas and serve it via Swagger UI.

stories.title: Generate OpenAPI 3.1 spec and serve Swagger UI
stories.description: Install @fastify/swagger and @fastify/swagger-ui, configure them with the API title and version, and verify all routes appear in the generated spec.
completion_criteria: GET /documentation returns an HTML page with Swagger UI that lists all implemented endpoints
The generated spec includes request and response schemas for every route

stories.title: Add example requests and responses to all endpoints
stories.description: Annotate each route definition with example request bodies and example responses so the Swagger UI shows realistic sample data.
completion_criteria: Every POST and PATCH endpoint shows at least one example request body in Swagger UI
Every endpoint shows at least one example success response and one example error response
